caseStudies.initialProfile: Child only used a smartphone. Hunting-and-pecking keys at 8 WPM. No understanding of computer files or system structures.

milestones.week2: Understood the home row and home keys. Stopped looking at the keyboard.
milestones.week4: Mastered top and bottom rows. Speed reached 20 WPM.
milestones.week6: Graduated at 35 WPM with 96% accuracy. Built a neat folder structure for school homework files.

caseStudies.finalOutcome: Child is completely independent in opening, naming, organizing, and saving homework documents on the family laptop.

caseStudies.initialProfile: High screen time spent playing mobile shooter games. Had no logical coding experience. Parents wanted active creative screen time.

milestones.week2: Understood grid movements and loop statements. Built a sprite follower puzzle.
milestones.week4: Learned X/Y coordinates and variable counters. Designed a custom scorekeeper.
milestones.week6: Created a multi-level gravity game. Shared game link with peers.

caseStudies.finalOutcome: Successfully shift of interest from playing games to programming game mechanics. Expressed interest in learning Python next.

caseStudies.initialProfile: Familiar with basic text typing but struggled with organizing science project charts or using cell calculations.

milestones.week2: Mastered table column grids and cell ranges. Wrote basic addition formulas.
milestones.week4: Learned math average functions and automatic sort commands. Designed clean school tables.
milestones.week6: Mastered auto charts creation (bar, pie charts) and Google drive sharing controls.

caseStudies.finalOutcome: Created a clean math data spreadsheet and presented it visually in a PowerPoint template, saving hours of manual plotting.
